purpose: Generate pronounceable nonsense wrappers for relation-validation items while preserving underlying logical form.

examples: Tiven is less dax than Nalo.

directional_pair.pairing_rule: rootb is the linked converse of roota

set_relation.note: Use root placeholders only as abstract relation labels.

relation_templates.query_template: Which option expresses the same underlying relation?
relation_templates.good_option_rule: Preserve argument-role assignment under the linked converse or equivalent surface form.
foil_rules: use correct form with unrelated entities

relation_templates.query_template: Select all options that instantiate the target relation.
relation_templates.good_option_rule: Each correct option independently satisfies the target logical form.

relation_templates.query_template: Which option satisfies the target relation?
relation_templates.good_option_rule: Option must satisfy the relation directly, not merely mention the same tokens.
foil_rules: duplicate argument where distinctness is required
mirror the relation without the needed converse

binding_load.1: single binary relation with two arguments
binding_load.2: binary relation with near-miss distractors or multiple candidate options
binding_load.3: three-argument or chained relations; may require preserving two linked bindings
binding_load.4: graph-like or role-mapping structures with multiple linked bindings

uncertainty_level.3: several near-miss rivals or overlapping cues

control_burden.3: multiple near-miss distractors, wrapper shift, or time pressure
control_burden.4: mixed wrappers, multi-select, and cross-item interference

tier_progression.description: Binary relation only, one premise, one correct option, real-world wrapper, no multi-select.
Binary relation with converse mapping; 3-4 options; same entities reused across foils.
Wrapper swap begins; multi-select possible; abstract/nonsense surface forms introduced.
Three-argument or chained relations, e.g. all_different(A,B,C) or earlier_than(A,B)&earlier_than(B,C).

adaptive_rules.design_goal: Adapt like the n-back: fixed family kernel, then wrapper variation, then speed, then load.

post_block_decision.UP: accuracy >= .85 and no late collapse; introduce wrapper swap first, then fast mode, then higher tier/binding load
post_block_decision.HOLD: .70 <= accuracy < .85 or mixed stability; keep tier and optionally rotate surface tokens
post_block_decision.DOWN: accuracy < .70 or strong collapse; reduce tier, restore familiar wrapper, and reduce distractor burden

error_sensitive_routing.role_reversal_errors: increase same_relation_mcq items with converse traps
error_sensitive_routing.polarity_errors: keep same entities but simplify operator polarity
error_sensitive_routing.multi_select_overreach: reduce option count and return to single-choice
error_sensitive_routing.wrapper_cost_high: repeat same logical form in new real-world wrapper before full nonsense swap
